response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned polyglot engineer with genuine strengths in developer tooling, large-scale systems, and software craftsmanship, but his experience is fundamentally misaligned with the core requirements of this role. The position demands deep Java and Kotlin expertise, strong Spring ecosystem knowledge, and JVM internals fluency — none of which are meaningfully evidenced in his resume. His primary stack is TypeScript, DLang, and Node.js; Java appears only in passing references (robotic arms at Altom, skills list), with no Kotlin or Spring mentioned anywhere. His NI LabWindows experience (C/C++ IDE tooling) and recent MCP tooling work show an interest in developer tooling, but these are insufficient substitutes for the JVM-specific depth this role demands.
strengths: Hands-on developer tooling and IDE-adjacent experience (NI LabWindows C IDE, MCP code generators, agentic frameworks at Naver)
Demonstrated ability to work on large-scale, high-throughput systems (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests/second with low-latency async I/O)
Consistent application of software craftsmanship (TDD, pairing, trunk-based development, CI/CD) across multiple senior roles
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin depth — the foundational JVM languages for this role appear only superficially in the skills list and one brief role mention
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework experience across 15+ years of work history
Primary technical identity is TypeScript/DLang/Node.js — a fundamentally different ecosystem from the JVM tooling stack this position requires

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
